'''Matt "Matty" Blaymire''' (born 4 June 1982) is an English former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 2000s and 2010s. He played at club level for [[York City Knights]] and [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] as a {{rlp|fb}}.

Blaymire was born in [[Leeds]], [[West Yorkshire]], England on 4 June 1982. He started his [[rugby league]] career in the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] academy before joining [[York City Knights]] in 2003, where he scored 22 tries in 50 appearances.<ref>{{cite web|last1=Martini|first1=Peter|title=York City Knights in need of more nous power, says Dave Woods|url=http://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/9240328.Knights_in_need_of_more_nous_power__says_Dave_Woods|website=York Press|accessdate=21 May 2015|date=8 September 2011}}</ref> After impressing for York, Blaymire returned to the [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]] at the end of the 2006's [[Super League XI]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Wildcats sign Golden and Blaymire|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/wakefield/5372490.stm|website=BBC Sport|access-date=21 May 2015|date=22 September 2006}}</ref>  He spent five seasons at [[Wakefield Trinity|Wakefield Trinity Wildcats]], making over 100 appearances for the club before being forced to retire in 2011 due to a knee injury.<ref>{{cite web|title=Wakefield full-back Matthew Blaymire set to retire|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/14829822|website=BBC Sport|access-date=21 May 2015|date=7 September 2011}}</ref>
